clinical_history: This ECG is most likely to be associated with what arrhythmia?
diagnosis: c) The patient had been resuscitated from a cardiac arrest,  and this post-arrest ECG revealed marked QT(U) prolongation.  The long  QT was multifactorial here, associated with tricyclic use, anorexia  nervosa, and probable hypokalemia.  There are T-U waves best seen in  V3 and V4.  Echocardiogram showed normal ventricular function and size,  despite prominent voltage in this thin adult female.
